info.title: Streaming Availability API
info.description:
Movie of the Night's Streaming Availability API on RapidAPI — answers 'where can I watch this movie?' for a given country. Used by the movie pack to filter recommendations down to titles the user can actually stream right now.

Docs: https://docs.movieofthenight.com/. Auth: API key in `X-RapidAPI-Key` header (deployment-level, set OMDB-style env var `X_RAPIDAPI_KEY`).

get.summary: Where a single show can be streamed.
get.description: Looks up a show by IMDb id (cheapest path — same id you already have on the Movie record from OMDb) and returns the full per-country streaming options array. Always pass the `country` query param to filter down to the user's market — otherwise the response is enormous (every supported country) and most of it is irrelevant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: IMDb id including the leading 'tt', e.g. 'tt0110912'. Same value as Movie.imdbId.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, lowercase, e.g. 'us', 'gb', 'au'. The user's country — recall it from their profile via DICE; if unknown, ASK them, don't guess.

parameters.name: series_granularity
parameters.description: For series only — 'show' (default), 'season', or 'episode'. Movies ignore this.

200.description: Full show record including the per-country `streamingOptions` map.

404.description: No show found with that id.

get.summary: Search for shows by title within a single country's catalogue.
get.description: Use when you only have a title and need both the streaming-availability record and an IMDb id. Returns up to 25 hits per call; results are scoped to ONE country (passed via `country`). Prefer `getShow` whenever you already have an IMDb id from OMDb — it's one call instead of search-then-get.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, lowercase. Same as `getShow`.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Title or substring of the title to search for.

parameters.name: show_type
parameters.description: Restrict to 'movie' or 'series'. Default is both.

Show.description: A movie or series with per-country streaming availability. Only the fields the recommend-movie skill actually consumes are typed below; the API returns more.

id.description: Streaming Availability's own id, NOT the IMDb id.

imdbId.description: IMDb id including the 'tt' prefix. Use this to join back to the Movie record.

streamingOptions.description: Map keyed by ISO country code. Each value is an array of streaming options (one per service × type). When you call with `country=us` you get only the `us` key — that's the array to read.

StreamingOption.description: One way the user can watch this show on one service. The user might have access to several of these (different services, different prices); pick the cheapest matching their subscriptions.

id.description: Service id, e.g. 'netflix', 'prime', 'disney'. Match against the user's known subscriptions.

type.description: How the user watches it. Prefer 'subscription' / 'free' over 'rent' / 'buy' when surfacing recommendations.

link.description: Direct deep-link to the title on the service. Render as a markdown link in chat.

videoLink.description: Trailer or preview link, when present.

price.description: Present only on rent/buy. Subscription/free options have no price.

expiresOn.description: Unix epoch seconds. When the title leaves this service, if known.
